Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Don't use iface as both a interface and IP string. Add a new NID argument instead.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

Honestly I would make this the base patch of this work since when using the Netlink API it is really easy to add this. I would add a new option to lnetctl net add / del like --nid. Then pass in that nidstr to yaml_lnet_config_ni() and add it to the YAML document
(-nid: 10.0.0.2@tcp). One the kernel side add handling the nid in the function lnet_genl_parse_local_ni(). Once you the struct lnet_nid you pass that to lnet_dyn_add_ni()
which would have to be updated to handle having the address. I invited to the slack channel so if you need help just ask.

Patch Set 3:

Thank you. I will get back to it soon and I would appreciate some help regarding netlink.

Patch Set 3:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

Now that LU-10003 patch landed we can easily update this.

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

commit.subject: LU-13642 lnet: Allow dynamic IP specification
commit.message:
LU-13642 lnet: Allow dynamic IP specification

This change is for the lnetctl app for dynamic configuration.

It allows selecting an interface by specifying an IP address.
These variations of interface and IP address are considered.

1 interface and no IP: Select main IP address
2 no interface and IP specified: Select first interface
         that has the IP address
3 interface and IP specified: Verify that interface and IP match

For further reference please read

IP specification in LNet
https://wiki.whamcloud.com/display/LNet/IP+specification+in+LNet

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I003a227418e6b8036f1c5bc76523fb7644563162
